reasoning: The CBOE Crude Oil Volatility Index (OVX) closed at 38.7 on 2025-10-24, an elevated level compared to its historical median (around 34). Volatility indices are historically mean-reverting. The recent spike up to 40.46 has already begun reversing. Over a short horizon (Nov 02), momentum strongly favors a further decline, making it unlikely to increase relative to 38.7. Over medium (Nov 25) and longer (Jan 24) horizons, predicting volatility becomes more difficult as the chance of new, unexpected geopolitical shocks disrupting the oil market increases. Consequently, uncertainty bounds widen and the probability gradually trends closer to 0.5 for distant dates, strictly adhering to standard forecasting bounds on uncertainty.
